title: Czavi'er Hill & Clifton "Cliff" Baxter Investigation — March 22–25, 2022

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, Va. Code §§ 2.2-3700 through 2.2-3715, I respectfully request access to the following multimedia records related to the March 2022 fire incidents involving Czavi'er Hill, the death investigation of Clifton "Cliff" Baxter, and the subsequent investigation of Cola Winborne Beale IV:
Requested Records:

1. Body-worn camera video and audio, in-car/dash camera (MVR) video and audio, and any other video or audio recordings made by Virginia Beach Police Department personnel who responded to the structure fire and vehicle fire at Czavi'er Hill's residence on or about March 24, 2022 on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, Virginia — from initial arrival through scene clearance.
2. Body-worn camera video and audio, in-car/dash camera (MVR) video and audio, and any other video or audio recordings made by Virginia Beach Police Department personnel who conducted the welfare check and subsequent death investigation at the residence of Clifton "Cliff" Baxter on or about March 25, 2022 in Virginia Beach, Virginia — from initial arrival through scene clearance.
3. Crime scene photographs (digital still images) taken by Virginia Beach Police Department forensic, crime scene, or investigative personnel at Czavi'er Hill's residence and associated vehicle fire scene on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, on or about March 24, 2022.
4. Crime scene video walkthrough(s) recorded by Virginia Beach Police Department forensic, crime scene, or investigative personnel at Czavi'er Hill's residence and associated vehicle fire scene on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, on or about March 24, 2022 (if created).
5. Crime scene photographs (digital still images) taken by Virginia Beach Police Department forensic, crime scene, or investigative personnel at Clifton "Cliff" Baxter's residence in Virginia Beach, Virginia, on or about March 25, 2022.
6. Crime scene video walkthrough(s) recorded by Virginia Beach Police Department forensic, crime scene, or investigative personnel at Clifton "Cliff" Baxter's residence in Virginia Beach, Virginia, on or about March 25, 2022 (if created).
7. Any Ring doorbell video and/or third-party residential or business surveillance video obtained, downloaded, or copied by Virginia Beach Police Department personnel in connection with this investigation, including original files as received and any VBPD-created copies, clips, or edited versions preserved as evidence.
8. 911 audio of any call(s) placed by callers (names unknown) on March 22–24, 2022, reporting fire(s) and/or shots fired at or near Czavi'er Hill's residence on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, Virginia.
9. Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) call-for-service logs (multimedia/audio outputs only, if any audio dispatch recordings are maintained) associated with the fire and/or shots-fired calls at or near Czavi'er Hill's residence on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, on March 22–24, 2022.
10. 911 audio of the call placed by the caller (name unknown — requestor for welfare check) on or about March 25, 2022, requesting a welfare check at Clifton "Cliff" Baxter's residence in Virginia Beach, Virginia.
11. Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) call-for-service logs (multimedia/audio outputs only, if any audio dispatch recordings are maintained) associated with the welfare check and discovery of Clifton "Cliff" Baxter on or about March 25, 2022 at Baxter's residence in Virginia Beach, Virginia.
12. Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s), and any other recorded audio or video statements, of Cola Winborne Beale IV conducted by the Virginia Beach Police Department in connection with this investigation.

Incident Details:

Case/Incident Number: Not available in Public Records
Victim: Clifton "Cliff" Baxter
Related Individual: Czavi'er Hill (fire/vehicle fire incident)
Subject: Cola Winborne Beale IV
Date of Incidents: March 22–25, 2022 (fires on or about March 24, 2022; welfare check/death investigation on or about March 25, 2022)
Location of Incidents: Czavi'er Hill's residence on Baccalaureate Drive, Virginia Beach, Virginia; and Clifton "Cliff" Baxter's residence, Virginia Beach, Virginia

This request seeks the native digital copies of the above-listed multimedia records as maintained by your agency. I am not requesting written reports, case files, or transcripts at this time. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific legal basis under the Virginia FOIA and release all reasonably segregable portions.
Please provide an itemized cost estimate before processing. I prefer electronic delivery; however, if physical copies are necessary, please provide the records on a USB drive rather than CD. If your office does not maintain any of these records, please forward this request to the appropriate agency or advise me accordingly.
Thank you for your time and assistance.
Sincerely,
Keshav

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Danielle D..

title: DEA FOIA Request – Records Relating to OIP Remand Processing (Case No. 25-01822-F / Appeal A-2025-02534) Joshua Delo

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To: Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
FOIA/Privacy Act Unit

Request

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request the following records:

1. Custodian-Limited Communications (All Platforms)

Communications sent or received by Joshua L. Delo across email, text messages (SMS), Microsoft Teams, or other agency messaging platforms, that contain any of the following terms:

“25-01822-F”
“A-2025-02534”
“remand”
“duplicate”
“OIP”

Date Range: March 31, 2026 – April 1, 2026

2. Pre-Remand Coordination with OIP (30-Day Window)

Records sufficient to show:

The identity of DEA personnel who communicated with DOJ Office of Information Policy (OIP) regarding the appeal prior to the March 31, 2026 remand
Any communications reflecting agreement, representation, or understanding that the requested search could or would be completed upon remand

Date Range: March 1, 2026 – March 31, 2026

Formats Included:

Emails
Text messages
Microsoft Teams or similar messaging platforms
Call logs, meeting invites, or notes reflecting phone conversations

3. Remand Decision Documentation

Records to sufficient to show:

The individual(s) responsible for determining that the remanded request would be administratively closed as a “duplicate”
The basis for that determination
Any written instruction, policy, or internal guidance relied upon

4. Communications with DOJ Office of Information Policy (OIP)

Records of communications between DEA personnel and DOJ OIP limited to the March 31, 2026 remand, including:

Emails
Text messages
Microsoft Teams or similar messaging platforms
Call logs or notes reflecting phone conversations

5. FOIA Case Processing Records (Audit Trail)

Records sufficient to show:

Case activity logs
Status changes
Workflow entries or internal notes

For Case No. 25-01822-F
Date Range: March 31, 2026 – April 2, 2026

6. Review or Feedback Related to Remand Handling

Records sufficient to show whether any:

Supervisory review
Feedback
Corrective action

…was issued specifically regarding the handling of the March 31, 2026 remand

Records Systems to Be Searched

This request includes, but is not limited to:

DEA enterprise email systems
Microsoft Teams or equivalent messaging platforms
Agency-issued mobile device text messaging systems (SMS/iMessage or equivalent, if preserved)
DEA FOIA case management and tracking systems
Calendar systems (e.g., Outlook)
Any system used to log FOIA workflow activity or case notes
Date Range

March 1, 2026 – April 2, 2026

Requested Action
Conduct a de novo review
Release all reasonably segregable portions
Provide a Vaughn index for any withheld material
Identify all custodians, systems, and communication platforms searched
Format

Electronic (.pdf), via email

Fee Waiver

Requested. Disclosure is in the public interest as it concerns agency compliance with FOIA obligations and representations made to DOJ OIP during the appellate process.

Expedited Processing

Requested due to:

Documented remand issued March 31, 2026
Immediate administrative closure on April 1, 2026
Potential inconsistency between pre-remand representations and post-remand actions

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes
